chunks.title: Vendor Quality Audit
chunks.text: Facility vendor quality audit checklist for high-rise residential towers.

chunks.title: Vendor Quality Audit
chunks.text: Auditors verify plumbing response times, electrical isolation procedures, and lift maintenance logs during quarterly reviews.

chunks.title: Vendor Quality Audit
chunks.text: Repeat failures in the same vertical stack must be escalated to the project engineering head and recorded in the audit management system.

chunks.title: Amc Scope
chunks.text: Annual maintenance contract scope summary for residential towers covered under developer AMC.

chunks.title: Amc Scope
chunks.text: Project-side defects during defect liability period remain with the developer project team until formal handover sign-off. Facility management handles routine wear and resident-caused damage after handover.

chunks.title: Amc Scope
chunks.text: Lift and firefighting systems may have separate OEM contracts; verify contract owner before dispatching vendors.

chunks.title: Facilities Steering
chunks.text: Minutes of facilities steering committee — seepage and waterproofing backlog.

chunks.title: Facilities Steering
chunks.text: The committee noted recurring ceiling seepage complaints in monsoon season and agreed to prioritize structural waterproofing inspections where adjacent flats show correlated categories.

chunks.title: Facilities Steering
chunks.text: Vendor coordination between plumbing and civil teams is required before closing tickets marked as structural envelope issues.

chunks.title: Community Guidelines
chunks.text: Community guidelines for reporting maintenance issues.

chunks.title: Community Guidelines
chunks.text: Residents should report water leaks with clear photos and avoid combining unrelated trades in a single ticket when issues require different specialist skills.

chunks.title: Community Guidelines
chunks.text: Noise-related complaints after hours route to security first; structural concerns should reference tower and flat clearly for pattern detection across floors.

chunks.title: Electrical Isolation
chunks.text: Electrical safety bulletin for facility technicians.

chunks.title: Electrical Isolation
chunks.text: Sparking distribution boards and burning smells require immediate isolation at the feeder and notification to security. Do not route routine electrician visits when shock or fire risk keywords are present — escalate to specialist electrical safety response.

chunks.title: Electrical Isolation
chunks.text: High voltage common area faults must be treated as priority one until cleared by a licensed supervisor.

chunks.title: Seepage Response
chunks.text: Standard operating procedure: resident seepage complaints.

chunks.title: Seepage Response
chunks.text: When seepage is reported from the ceiling, capture photographs, check flat history for prior plumbing tickets, and coordinate with the floor above before assigning only a plumber.

chunks.title: Seepage Response
chunks.text: If multiple floors in the same column show water ingress within ninety days, invoke the building pattern review and consider structural waterproofing hypothesis.
